title: Quiz: Quantum Advantage in Finance
description: Test your understanding of quantum computing applications in finance

questions.question: What speedup does quantum amplitude estimation provide over classical Monte Carlo?
questions.explanation: Quantum amplitude estimation provides quadratic speedup, reducing the number of samples needed from O(1/ε²) to O(1/ε) for a given accuracy ε.

questions.question: What does NISQ stand for?
questions.explanation: NISQ stands for Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum, referring to current quantum computers with 50-1000+ noisy qubits without full error correction.

questions.question: Which quantum algorithm is best suited for combinatorial optimization problems like asset selection?
questions.explanation: QAOA is designed for combinatorial optimization problems and is well-suited for discrete financial problems like asset selection from a universe of options.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of hybrid quantum-classical algorithms?

options.text: They don't require any quantum hardware
They work around NISQ limitations by combining quantum and classical resources
They are faster than pure quantum algorithms

questions.explanation: Hybrid algorithms use quantum computers for hard computational kernels while handling optimization, error mitigation, and other tasks classically, working around current hardware limitations.

questions.question: All financial problems will benefit from quantum computing.
questions.explanation: False. Quantum advantage depends on problem structure. Many financial problems can be solved efficiently classically, and quantum computers only provide advantages for specific problem classes.

questions.question: If a quantum circuit has 100 gates and each gate has 99.5% fidelity, approximately what is the circuit success probability?
questions.explanation: Success probability = 0.995^100 ≈ 0.606 or about 60%. This illustrates why noise is such a significant challenge.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key financial application of quantum amplitude estimation?
questions.explanation: Quantum amplitude estimation can speed up Monte Carlo methods used for option pricing, Value at Risk, and other expectation calculations.

questions.question: Which are current limitations of NISQ quantum computers? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Inability to perform any useful calculations

questions.explanation: NISQ computers face noise, limited qubits, and imperfect gates. However, they CAN perform useful calculations, especially with hybrid algorithms and error mitigation.
